1	\chapter{Higher direct images $R^i f_*$ of quasi-coherent sheaves ($i \geq 1$)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean
4	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, definition of $R^i f_*$ as the
5	% $i$-th right derived functor of $f_*$ (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex
6	% and references/stacks-coherent.md).
7	
8	\section{Setup and motivation}
9	
10	This chapter records the derived-functor higher direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\)
11	for \(i \geq 1\). It provides the single object that the {\v C}ech computation of
12	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage} compares against: the comparison
13	theorem \(\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}\) identifies the cohomology of the
14	relative {\v C}ech complex with \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) wherever the derived functor
15	is defined.
16	
17	We use the classical cohomology-sheaf formulation (not the derived category).
18	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
19	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
20	
21	\section{Definition of the higher direct images}
22	
23	\begin{definition}\leanok
24	[Higher direct image]
25	  \label{def:higher_direct_image}
26	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage}
27	  % NOTE (iter-233): the Lean `higherDirectImage` carries an explicit instance
28	  % hypothesis `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`, because Mathlib has
29	  % `Abelian X.Modules` but NOT `EnoughInjectives` / `IsGrothendieckAbelian` for
30	  % `SheafOfModules` over a sheaf of rings (the Grothendieck-abelian instance is
31	  % only for `Sheaf J A` with a fixed value category). The definition is therefore
32	  % conditional; the hypothesis can be dropped once a lane proves that instance.
33	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
34	  % `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing' (proof), describing $R^i f_*$ as the
35	  % $i$-th right derived functor of $f_*$ via the sheafified presheaf
36	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$
37	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L188--192).
38	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "According to
39	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
40	  % the sheaf
41	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
42	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$."
43	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Quasi-coherence of
44	  higher direct images (and the underlying Cohomology chapter).}
45	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
46	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. For \(i \geq 0\) the \emph{\(i\)-th
47	  higher direct image} \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(i\)-th right derived functor
48	  of the pushforward functor \(f_*\) applied to \(\mathcal{F}\). Concretely,
49	  \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module obtained as the
50	  sheafification of the presheaf
51	  \[
52	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^i\bigl(f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
53	    \qquad V \subset S \text{ open},
54	  \]
55	  where \(H^i(-, -)\) denotes sheaf cohomology. For \(i = 0\) this recovers the
56	  ordinary pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).
57	\end{definition}

1021	  extended complex is identically \(0\). If \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\) then
1022	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) and, by the explicit
1023	  description of the free presheaves \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)(W) =
1024	  \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise, \(K(\mathcal{U})_p(W) =
1025	  \bigoplus_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) with the alternating-sum
1026	  differential. Fixing an index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in I_1\), the prescription
1027	  \[
1028	    h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} =
1029	      \begin{cases}
1030	        s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p+1}} & i_0 = i_{\mathrm{fix}}, \\
1031	        0 & i_0 \neq i_{\mathrm{fix}}
1032	      \end{cases}
1033	  \]
1034	  defines a contracting homotopy: a direct computation gives \((dh + hd)(s) = s\),
1035	  so the identity of \(K^{\mathrm{ext}}_\bullet(W)\) is homotopic to zero. Hence the
1036	  extended complex is exact over every \(W\), which is the claimed vanishing of the
1037	  homology presheaves.
1038	\end{proof}

1040	\begin{lemma}[Right adjoint of a mono-preserving left adjoint preserves injectives]
1041	  \label{lem:injective_of_adjoint}

1046	  Let \(L \dashv R\) be an adjunction between abelian (or, more generally, suitable)
1047	  categories such that the left adjoint \(L\) preserves monomorphisms. Then for every
1048	  injective object \(J\) of the target, \(R(J)\) is an injective object of the source.
1049	\end{lemma}

1057	  For the identity ring map \(\alpha = \mathbf{1}\) over a scheme \(X\),
1058	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion

1060	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules:

1114	% (or its proof) to see the result."
1115	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let $\mathcal{I}$ be an injective presheaf of
1116	% $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.

1120	% $$

1122	% =

1126	% quasi-isomorphic to $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}[0]$. Hence by

1130	\begin{proof}
1131	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:section_cech_complex, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification, lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso, lem:injective_of_adjoint, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
1132	  The argument has two independent parts.
1133	
1134	  \emph{Part 1: an injective sheaf is injective as a presheaf.} Sheafification is
1135	  left adjoint to the inclusion
1136	  \(\iota : \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1137	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of
1138	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), and its left
1139	  adjoint sheafification is exact, hence preserves monomorphisms. By
1140	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} the right adjoint \(\iota\) carries injective
1141	  objects to injective objects; thus an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
1142	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as an object of \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). In
1143	  particular the contravariant functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-,
1144	  \mathcal{I})\) is exact on \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
1145	
1146	  \emph{Part 2: vanishing.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} the free
1147	  presheaf complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of
1148	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} is a resolution of
1149	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\); equivalently the
1150	  augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to
1151	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
1152	  Applying the exact functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})\)
1153	  of Part 1 to this exact augmented complex yields an exact complex; by the
1154	  identification \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet,
1155	  \mathcal{I}) = \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I})\) of
1156	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification} (with the section {\v C}ech complex
1157	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}), this is precisely the statement that
1158	  the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\mathcal{I}\) is exact in positive degrees, with
1159	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}, \mathcal{I}) =
1160	  \mathcal{I}(U)\) supplying degree \(0\). Hence
1161	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and
1162	  \(\check{H}^0(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = \mathcal{I}(U)\). This direct route uses
1163	  neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1164	  \(\delta\)-functor identification.
1165	\end{proof}

1179	  % be a short exact sequence of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.

1186	  \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}.}
1187	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and let
1188	  \[
1189	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0

1192	  open. If there is a cofinal system of open coverings \(\mathcal{U}\) of \(U\) with
1193	  \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\), then the map on sections

1215	% $$

1221	  Let \(s \in \mathcal{H}(U)\). Choose an open covering \(\mathcal{U} : U =
1222	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) fine enough that (a) \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U},
1223	  \mathcal{F}) = 0\) and (b) each restriction \(s|_{U_i}\) lifts to a section
1224	  \(s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\); both can be arranged simultaneously because the
1225	  coverings with property (a) are cofinal and (b) holds on any sufficiently fine
1226	  cover (surjectivity of \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H}\) is a local condition).

1233	  \(\mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 i_1})\); the family \((s_{i_0 i_1})\) is a {\v C}ech

1326	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{B}\) be a basis for its topology, let
1327	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and suppose there is a set
1328	  \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of open coverings such that: (1) every \(\mathcal{U} :

1331	  \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(U\) occurring in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are
1332	  cofinal among all open coverings of \(U\); and (3) for every \(\mathcal{U} \in
1333	  \mathrm{Cov}\) the {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes,

1335	  \[

1337	      U \in \mathcal{B}.

1340	  vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} to the Serre vanishing of

1342	  opens and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) as the standard affine covers, condition (3) is exactly

1393	% H^1(U, \mathcal{Q}) \ar[lld] \\
1394	% &
1395	% \ldots & \ldots & \ldots \\
1396	% }
1397	% $$
1398	% for any $U \in \mathcal{B}$. Since $\mathcal{I}$ is injective we
1399	% have $H^n(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0$ for $n > 0$ (see
1400	% Derived Categories, Lemma \ref{derived-lemma-higher-derived-functors}).
1401	% By the above we see that $H^0(U, \mathcal{I}) \to H^0(U, \mathcal{Q})$
1402	% is surjective and hence $H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$.
1403	% Since $\mathcal{F}$ was an arbitrary $\mathcal{O}_X$-module with
1404	% vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for all $\mathcal{U} \in \text{Cov}$
1405	% we conclude that also $H^1(U, \mathcal{Q}) = 0$ since $\mathcal{Q}$ is
1406	% another of these sheaves (see above). By the long exact sequence this in
1407	% turn implies that $H^2(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$. And so on and so forth."
1408	\begin{proof}
1409	  \uses{lem:injective_cech_acyclic, lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
1410	  Say that an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module has \emph{vanishing higher {\v C}ech
1411	  cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\)} if condition (3) holds for it. We argue by a
1412	  dimension shift, taking \(\mathcal{F}\) (with vanishing higher {\v C}ech
1413	  cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\)) as the inductive variable and using no input from
1414	  affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing.
1415	
1416	  Embed \(\mathcal{F} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{I}\) into an injective
1417	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\). By
1418	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} the injective \(\mathcal{I}\) has
1419	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and every cover, in
1420	  particular for every \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\). Put \(\mathcal{Q} =
1421	  \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), giving the short exact sequence
1422	  \[
1423	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0.
1424	  \]
1425	  For each \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are cofinal among
1426	  coverings of \(U\) (condition (2)) and \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
1427	  0\) for them, so Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} makes the sequence exact on sections,
1428	  \[
1429	    0 \to \mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U) \to 0,
1430	    \qquad U \in \mathcal{B}.
1431	  \]
1432	  Since by condition (1) every term of a {\v C}ech complex for a cover
1433	  \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) is a product of values over elements of
1434	  \(\mathcal{B}\), this section-level exactness yields a short exact sequence of
1435	  {\v C}ech complexes
1436	  \[
1437	    0 \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \to
1438	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) \to
1439	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) \to 0,
1440	  \]
1441	  and hence a long exact sequence of {\v C}ech cohomology. As
1442	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
1443	  0\) for \(p > 0\), the long exact sequence forces \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U},
1444	  \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\): the quotient \(\mathcal{Q}\) again has
1445	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\).
1446	
1447	  Now run the sheaf-cohomology long exact sequence of \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to
1448	  \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) over a fixed \(U \in \mathcal{B}\). The
1449	  injective \(\mathcal{I}\) has \(H^n(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(n > 0\). The
1450	  section-level surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(U) = H^0(U, \mathcal{I}) \to H^0(U,
1451	  \mathcal{Q}) = \mathcal{Q}(U)\) established above makes the connecting map into
1452	  \(H^1(U, \mathcal{F})\) zero, whence \(H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). Applying the
1453	  same conclusion to \(\mathcal{Q}\) (another module with vanishing higher {\v C}ech
1454	  cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\)) gives \(H^1(U, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\), and then the
1455	  long exact sequence with \(H^1(U, \mathcal{I}) = H^2(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) forces
1456	  \(H^2(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). Iterating the dimension shift — at each stage
1457	  \(\mathcal{Q}\) plays the role of \(\mathcal{F}\) — gives \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) =
1458	  0\) for all \(p > 0\) and every \(U \in \mathcal{B}\).
1459	
1460	  The argument never uses affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing
1461	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}); its only {\v C}ech-vanishing input is
1462	  condition (3), which on the affine instantiation is supplied by the standard-cover
1463	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. Thus the dependency
1464	  \(\text{affine Serre vanishing} \to \text{this lemma}\) is one-directional and the
1465	  graph is acyclic.
1466	\end{proof}

1468	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
1469	
1470	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
1471	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
1472	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
1473	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
1474	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
1475	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
1476	
1477	\begin{lemma}
1478	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
1479	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
1480	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
1481	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
1482	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1483	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
1484	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
1485	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
1486	  % \begin{equation}
1487	  % \label{equation-extended}
1488	  % 0 \to
1489	  % M \to
1490	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
1491	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
1492	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
1493	  % \ldots
1494	  % \end{equation}
1495	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
1496	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
1497	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
1498	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
1499	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
1500	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
1501	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
1502	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
1503	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
1504	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
1505	  \[
1506	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
1507	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
1508	    \qquad
1509	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1510	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
1511	  \]
1512	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
1513	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
1514	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
1515	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
1516	\end{lemma}
1517	
1518	\begin{proof}
1519	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
1520	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
1521	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
1522	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
1523	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
1524	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
1525	  localisations
1526	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
1527	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
1528	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
1529	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
1530	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
1531	\end{proof}

1533	\begin{lemma}
1534	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
1535	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
1536	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_isSheafify_presheafCohomology}
1537	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image}
1538	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
1539	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
1540	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
1541	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
1542	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
1543	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1544	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
1545	  % associated to the presheaves
1546	  % $$
1547	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
1548	  % $$
1549	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
1550	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
1551	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
1552	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
1553	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
1554	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
1555	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) of
1556	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has injective resolutions
1557	  (\([\,\mathrm{HasInjectiveResolutions}\;X.\mathrm{Modules}\,]\)), so that the
1558	  derived pushforward \(R^k f_* = (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\) is defined (see the
1559	  NOTE on Definition~\ref{def:higher_direct_image}). For every \(k \geq 0\) the
1560	  higher direct image
1561	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{G})\) is the sheaf
1562	  on \(S\) associated to the presheaf
1563	  \[
1564	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
1565	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
1566	  \]
1567	  with the evident restriction maps. In particular \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) vanishes
1568	  iff \(H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for every \(V\) in a basis of
1569	  the topology of \(S\); since the affine opens form such a basis, it suffices to
1570	  check the vanishing on affine \(V\). This is the local-on-the-base description
1571	  through which \(f_*\)-acyclicity is reduced to absolute cohomology vanishing on
1572	  preimages.
1573	\end{lemma}
1574	
1575	\begin{proof}
1576	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image}
1577	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
1578	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
1579	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\); since the abelian
1580	  structure on \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules computes cokernels and kernels objectwise on
1581	  the level of presheaves and then sheafifies, that cohomology sheaf is the
1582	  sheafification of the presheaf \(V \mapsto H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
1583	  = H^k\bigl(\mathcal{I}^\bullet(f^{-1}(V))\bigr) = H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\),
1584	  the last equality because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is an injective (hence
1585	  acyclic) resolution computing the absolute cohomology of \(f^{-1}(V)\). Evaluation
1586	  of a sheafification vanishes exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis,
1587	  giving the stated affine-local criterion.
1588	
1589	  The comparison identifying \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\) with the
1590	  sheafification of the cohomology presheaf \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V),
1591	  \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\) is constructed directly from injective resolutions
1592	  in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\): cohomology in \(S.\mathrm{Modules}\) is the
1593	  sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology, because the
1594	  sheafification/inclusion adjunction preserves the finite limits and colimits
1595	  computing kernels and cokernels. No presheaf-level \(\delta\)-functor formalism
1596	  is required.
1597	\end{proof}

1599	\begin{lemma}
1600	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
1601	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
1602	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
1603	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
1604	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1605	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
1606	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1607	  % L180--199).
1608	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
1609	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1610	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
1611	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
1612	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
1613	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
1614	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
1615	  \to S\). Then:
1616	  \begin{enumerate}
1617	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
1618	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
1619	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
1620	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
1621	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
1622	      \(k \geq 0\),
1623	      \[
1624	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
1625	      \]
1626	  \end{enumerate}
1627	\end{lemma}
1628	
1629	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
1630	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
1631	% the sheaf
1632	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
1633	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
1634	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
1635	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
1636	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
1637	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
1638	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
1639	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
1640	\begin{proof}
1641	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
1642	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
1643	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
1644	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
1645	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
1646	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
1647	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
1648	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
1649	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
1650	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
1651	
1652	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
1653	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
1654	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
1655	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
1656	
1657	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
1658	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
1659	  sheafification of
1660	  \[
1661	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
1662	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
1663	  \]
1664	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
1665	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
1666	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
1667	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
1668	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
1669	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
1670	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
1671	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
1672	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
1673	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
1674	
1675	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
1676	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
1677	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
1678	  for every \(k\),
1679	  \[
1680	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
1681	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
1682	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
1683	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
1684	  \]
1685	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
1686	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
1687	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
1688	\end{proof}

1690	\begin{lemma}
1691	[Each {\v C}ech term is $f_*$-acyclic]
1692	  \label{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}
1693	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic}
1694	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
1695	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1696	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
1697	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,

1699	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.

1702	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
1703	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative affine vanishing).}
1704	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be separated and quasi-compact, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
1705	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(\mathfrak{U}\) a finite affine open
1706	  cover of \(X\) with all intersections affine. Then for every \(p \geq 0\) the
1707	  degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech term \(\mathcal{C}^p =
1708	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\)
1709	  is right \((\,\cdot\,)_*\)-acyclic for the pushforward \(f_*\):
1710	  \[
1711	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0
1712	      \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1.

1715	
1716	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
1717	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
1718	% the sheaf
1719	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
1720	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
1721	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
1722	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
1723	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
1724	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
1725	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
1726	% opens we win."
1727	\begin{proof}
1728	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
1729	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
1730	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
1731	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
1732	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
1733	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
1734	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
1735	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
1736	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :

1754	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
1755	  \(k \geq 1\).

# Strategy

## Goal

Prove `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`),
the protected, frozen-signature target: for `f : X ⟶ S` separated and quasi-compact, `F`
quasi-coherent, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, an isomorphism in the weak
existence form `Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` (under
`[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`), where `higherDirectImage f i F =
((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`. End-state: zero inline `sorry` in the cone, zero
project axioms, kernel-only axioms. Extraction from the Algebraic-Jacobian challenge; the
downstream Picard/Quot machinery is out of scope and was carved away.

## Routes

### Route A — acyclic-resolution comparison (CHOSEN)
The augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` on `X` (`Cᵖ = ∏ (j_s)_*(F|_{U_s})`) is
(i) a resolution of `F` and (ii) termwise `(pushforward f)`-acyclic. The P4 abstract lemma
"a `G`-acyclic resolution computes `G.rightDerived`" then gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱf_* F`
directly — ONE abstract lemma, NO spectral sequences. The standard Cartan–Leray acyclic-cover
existence proof. Its acyclicity input (ii) reduces to affine Serre vanishing
`H^q(affine, qcoh)=0`, which is NOT free: see the bridge below.

### Route B — two spectral sequences (REJECTED, fallback only)
Čech-to-derived + Leray spectral sequences for `Scheme.Modules`. Rejected: both absent from
Mathlib (multi-thousand-LOC), and Leray needs quasi-coherence of `R^q f_* F`. Strictly heavier
than Route A for the same `Nonempty (…≅…)` goal. NOTE: Route B rests on the SAME irreducible
brick `injective_cech_acyclic` as Route A — rejecting B does not escape it. Fallback only.

### The acyclicity bridge (load-bearing, CORRECTED)
Route A's term-/relative-acyclicity inputs and the general-cover intersection vanishing all
reduce to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(Spec A, qcoh)=0` (Stacks 02KG). This is NOT obtainable
from the P3 contracting homotopy alone: the homotopy proves the Čech *complex* is exact (a
resolution), but term `G`-acyclicity is itself affine vanishing on a smaller affine — a circular
regress with no inductive base. The honest route (Stacks, torsor-free) is the minimal
Čech↔derived bridge P3b: (1) injective `O_X`-modules are Čech-acyclic
(`injective_cech_acyclic`); (2) `ses_cech_h1`; (3) the dimension-shift `cech_vanish_basis` (01EO)
consuming the standard-cover Čech vanishing of P3 as its condition (3). This yields
`affine_serre_vanishing` legitimately, breaking the cycle: P3 produces standard-cover Čech
vanishing; P3b lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing as an input.

**Direct route for (1)** (analogist `p3b-presheafcech`, iter-011): `injective_cech_acyclic` does NOT
need presheaf enough-injectives or the δ-functor universality. Two aligned parts: (a) injective
sheaf ⟹ injective presheaf via `Injective.injective_of_adjoint` applied to the
`Mod ↪ PMod` right adjoint (`sheafificationAdjunction`); (b) the free-presheaf complex
`K(𝒰)_• = ⨁ free(yoneda U_…)` resolves `O_𝒰` (objectwise contracting homotopy) and
`Hom(K_•,F) = Č•(𝒰,F)` (section complex) via `freeAdjunction`+Yoneda — so for injective `I`,
`Hom(-,I)` exact gives positive Čech vanishing. This BYPASSES the two expensive bricks
(`presheafModules_enoughInjectives`, `cech_delta_functor_presheaves` — both off the critical path,
dropped from the blueprint). The free-presheaf complex uses Mathlib's `PresheafOfModules.free`, NOT
a bespoke `j_!`. The FULL Stacks-01EO bootstrap (torsor `lemma-cech-h1`, `lemma-kill-cohomology-class`)
remains avoidable; only the injective-acyclicity brick is irreducible.

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage

/-!
# Čech acyclicity on affines — standard-cover specialisation (P3)

This file contains the P3 lemma: positive-degree vanishing of the Čech complex
of a standard affine open cover of an affine scheme.  The lemma is stated over
the spanning-family bundle `(s : ι → R, hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)`,
which simultaneously determines the cover (via
`Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop`) and the algebra-side exactness
certifier (`exact_of_isLocalized_span`).  The proof body is `sorry`; filling
it is the task of the P3 prover lane.

See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`,
Lemma `lem:cech_acyclic_affine`.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tags 02KG
(`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`) and
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules

/- Planner strategy (P3, see analogies/p3-localisation.md):

   L1 (gap-fill): identify `CechComplex` on this standard cover with the module
   complex `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` via `Γ(D(s_σ)) = M_{s_σ}` (Away localisation).  The
   key equation is `affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop_f i = Spec.map (algebraMap R
   (Localization.Away (s i)))`, so the sections over the `i`-th piece are exactly
   the away-localisation `M_{s_i} = IsLocalizedModule.Away (s i) M`.

   L2 (ALIGN): feed each positive-degree exactness node to
   `exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs`
   (`Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Exactness`), localising at the spanning
   elements `Away (s r)` (not at primes).  After L1, each degree-`p` term of the
   complex is `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` and the differential is the alternating Čech
   coboundary; `exact_of_isLocalized_span` reduces `Function.Exact d^{p-1} d^p`
   to the same exactness after inverting each `s_r` individually.

   L3 (gap-fill): in the localisation `A_{s_r}` the fixed index `i_fix = r`
   makes `s_r` invertible, so the contracting homotopy
   `h(σ)_{i₀…i_p} = σ_{r i₀…i_p}` is well-defined globally on `M_{s_r}`
   (no passage to a prime needed) and gives `Function.Exact` of the localised
   differentials via `dh + hd = id`.  Do NOT route through Mathlib's simplicial
   `ExtraDegeneracy` (wrong variance — chain vs. cochain — and no cosimplicial
   dual exists in Mathlib); instead prove the module homotopy directly.
-/

/-- **Standard-cover Čech-complex vanishing on affines** (P3; Stacks 02KG,
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).

Let `R` be a commutative ring, `s : ι → R` a spanning family
(`Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤`), and `F` a quasi-coherent `O_{Spec R}`-module.
The relative Čech complex of the standard affine cover
`Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs` has vanishing cohomology in all
positive degrees: `Hᵖ = 0` for `p ≥ 1`.

Route: (L1) identify the complex with `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` via away-localisation
sections; (L2) reduce positive-degree exactness to the localised complexes via
`exact_of_isLocalized_span`; (L3) supply the explicit contracting homotopy
`h(σ)_{i₀…i_p} = σ_{r i₀…i_p}` on `M_{s_r}` (where `s_r` is invertible). -/
theorem CechAcyclic.affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {S : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : Spec R ⟶ S) [IsAffineHom f]
    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
    (F : (Spec R).Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
    IsZero ((CechComplex f ((Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover) F).homology p) := by
  -- Reduction route (planner L1/L2/L3, `analogies/p3-localisation.md`):
  --   * L1 (categorical→module bridge, STILL MISSING): identify the abstract
